message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Driver rotation: Luca is Driver for Task #11">
Driver rotation complete. We're now working on Task #11: Implementation Step 1 — Navigation + Dashboard skeleton.

**Luca Palmieri** is the new Driver (the only agent who may modify files).
**Marty Cagan** has been respawned as a Reviewer.

Luca will be implementing US-GTD-8 from docs/user-stories.md. Please review his work as it progresses and send feedback to luca-palmieri from your area of expertise. Key areas to watch:
- Kent: TDD discipline, test-first approach
- Scott: Domain types for routes and navigation state
- Carson: HTMX patterns, hx-boost, route structure
- Lea: CSS for header nav, flexbox wrap, design tokens
- Steve Schoger: Visual hierarchy, spacing, nav design
- Steve Krug: Usability of navigation, trunk test
- Heydon: Nav landmark, aria-current, keyboard nav, skip link
- Marty: Match against US-GTD-8 acceptance criteria
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Understood. Luca is now the Driver for Task #11 (Navigation + Dashboard skeleton, US-GTD-8). I'm a Reviewer focused on TDD discipline.

Let me review the user story acceptance criteria to prepare my review lens.

file.content:
- [ ] User can edit a Waiting For item's title and waiting-on text
- [ ] User can delete a Waiting For item
- [ ] User can optionally link a Waiting For item to a project
- [ ] Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances

#### Accessibility
- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
- [ ] Waiting For list uses semantic `<ul>` so screen readers announce item count
- [ ] Each item's "waiting on" metadata is associated with the item (not just visual proximity)
- [ ] Resolve/convert/delete actions have accessible labels including the item title
- [ ] Focus management after resolving: focus moves to next item or empty state

#### Empty State
- "Nothing pending from others. When you delegate something or need to follow up,
  add it here." with link to add form.

#### Edge Cases
- Empty Waiting For list (show helpful message)
- Very long waiting-on description
- Resolving the last Waiting For item linked to a project

#### Success Metric
User can see everything they are waiting on in one glance.

---

### US-GTD-6: Someday/Maybe List

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** park ideas and future possibilities,

content.text: Let me get the full US-GTD-8 acceptance criteria.

content.content:
676:### US-GTD-8: Navigation and Dashboard
677-
678-**As a** GTD practitioner,
679-**I want to** navigate between all GTD lists and see a summary of my system's state,
680-**So that** I can access any part of my GTD system quickly and know what needs attention.
681-
682-#### Acceptance Criteria
683-
684-- [ ] Main navigation includes: Inbox (with count), Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For,
685-  Someday/Maybe, Weekly Review
686-- [ ] Dashboard/home page shows a summary:
687-  - Inbox count (items needing processing)
688-  - Stalled projects count (projects without next actions)
689-  - Total active next actions count
690-  - Waiting For count
691-- [ ] Navigation indicates the current page (aria-current="page")
692-- [ ] Navigation is accessible: keyboard operable, screen reader landmarks
693-- [ ] Logout remains accessible from every page
694-- [ ] Quick capture input is available on every authenticated page (in the header,
695-  above main content). Note: on the Inbox page, this header input and the inbox page's
696-  own capture input are the same form posting to the same endpoint -- avoid showing
697-  two capture inputs on the inbox page.
698-- [ ] Works without JavaScript
699-
700-#### Accessibility
701-- [ ] Navigation uses `<nav>` landmark with `aria-label="GTD lists"`
702-- [ ] Current page indicated with `aria-current="page"` on the active nav link
703-- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
704-- [ ] Inbox count badge is announced by screen readers (not just visual)
705-- [ ] Skip link jumps past header (including capture input) to main content
706-- [ ] Dashboard summary counts use semantic markup (e.g., `<dl>` for label/value pairs)
707-- [ ] Clear focus indicators on all navigation links
708-
709-#### Edge Cases
710-- All counts are zero (fresh user -- show getting started guidance)
711-- Very high counts (display doesn't break)
712-
713-#### Success Metric
714-Any list is reachable in one click from any page.
715-
716----
717-
718-## V1 GTD Priority and Implementation Order
719-
720-Following the walking skeleton principle. Clarify is split into sub-stories (US-GTD-2a
721-through 2e) and interleaved with the lists it routes to, so each clarify path is built
722-immediately after its destination list exists:
723-
724:1. **Navigation + Dashboard** (US-GTD-8) -- Multi-page structure, header nav, route skeleton
725-2. **Inbox Capture** (US-GTD-1) -- New entry point
726-3. **Contexts** -- Domain type + DB + default contexts for new users
727-4. **Next Actions with Contexts** (US-GTD-3) -- Core working list
728-5. **Clarify as Next Action + Trash** (US-GTD-2a, 2b) -- First clarify paths (inbox -> next action, inbox -> trash)
729-6. **Projects** (US-GTD-4) -- Multi-step outcome tracking
730-7. **Clarify as Project** (US-GTD-2d) -- Inbox -> new project + first next action
731-8. **Waiting For** (US-GTD-5) -- Delegation tracking
732-9. **Clarify as Waiting For** (US-GTD-2e) -- Inbox -> waiting for
733-10. **Someday/Maybe** (US-GTD-6) -- Idea parking
734-11. **Clarify as Someday/Maybe** (US-GTD-2c) -- Inbox -> someday/maybe
735-12. **Weekly Review** (US-GTD-7) -- The review process
736-13. **Data Migration + Cleanup** -- Migrate old todos, remove legacy code
737-
738-Each step follows the Atomic Green Step pipeline. Each step delivers a usable increment.
739-The clarify workflow is built incrementally -- each clarify path ships as soon as its
740-destination list is available.
741-
742----
743-
744-## Product Success Criteria (V1 GTD)
745-
746-V1 GTD is successful when:
747-
748-1. A user can capture items to their inbox and process them to zero
749-2. Clarified items land in the correct list (Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For,
750-   Someday/Maybe, or trash)
751-3. Next Actions are filterable by context
752-4. Every active project has at least one next action (stalled projects are flagged)
753-5. The Weekly Review guided flow keeps the system current
754-6. The entire system is accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA)
755-7. The system works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)
756-8. A first-time GTD user can understand the system without external instructions
757-9. All existing auth functionality continues to work
758-10. All tests pass (Rust integration + Playwright e2e + axe-core a11y)
759-
760-We will know this is working when a user can complete the full GTD loop (capture ->
761-clarify -> organize -> review -> engage) without hesitation.

content.text: Good. I have a clear picture of what US-GTD-8 requires. Now let me send Luca my TDD guidance for this first implementation step.

input.content:
Luca, Kent Beck here. Welcome to the Driver seat for Task #11 (Navigation + Dashboard skeleton, US-GTD-8). Here's my TDD guidance for this step.

## TDD Strategy for Nav + Dashboard

This is primarily a structural/UI task, not a domain logic task. That shifts where the tests live — more integration tests and e2e tests, fewer domain unit tests.

### What to Test-Drive

**Integration tests (tests/api/):**

1. `dashboard_redirects_unauthenticated_user_to_login` — GET / without session redirects to /login
2. `dashboard_returns_200_for_authenticated_user` — GET / with session returns 200 with HTML
3. `dashboard_shows_navigation_links` — response contains links to /inbox, /next-actions, /projects, /waiting-for, /someday-maybe, /review
4. `dashboard_shows_summary_counts` — response contains inbox count, next actions count, etc. (all zero for a fresh user)
5. `navigation_is_present_on_authenticated_pages` — spot-check that /inbox, /next-actions etc. all include the nav

For now, the GTD routes (/inbox, /next-actions, /projects, /waiting-for, /someday-maybe, /review) can be placeholder pages that return 200 with the nav and a "Coming soon" heading. The tests verify the route exists and has navigation — the content comes in later tasks.

**Playwright e2e:**

6. `authenticated_user_sees_dashboard_with_navigation` — login, verify dashboard loads, verify nav links are present and clickable
7. axe-core scan on the dashboard page

### What NOT to Test

- Don't test CSS styling (that's visual review territory)
- Don't test exact HTML structure (test behavior and content, not markup)
- Don't write domain unit tests for this task (no new domain types yet)

### Suggested Red-Green-Refactor Sequence

I'd recommend this order of small steps:

1. **Red**: Write `dashboard_redirects_unauthenticated_user_to_login` — fails because no dashboard route exists yet (currently / goes to the welcome page)
2. **Green**: Add a dashboard route that redirects unauthenticated users. Decide: does / become the dashboard, or does the welcome page remain for unauthenticated users and dashboard is at /dashboard?
3. **Red**: Write `dashboard_returns_200_for_authenticated_user` — fails because the authenticated route doesn't return a dashboard page yet
4. **Green**: Create the dashboard template with nav and summary area. Return it for authenticated users.
5. **Red**: Write `dashboard_shows_navigation_links` — assert the response contains hrefs for all GTD routes
6. **Green**: Add the nav links to the template
7. **Red**: Write a test for one placeholder route (e.g., `inbox_returns_200_for_authenticated_user`)
8. **Green**: Add the placeholder route and template
9. **Repeat** for each placeholder route
10. **Refactor**: Extract the nav into a shared template component (base.html block)

### Key Architectural Decision

The current base.html template has a minimal header. This task needs to evolve it into a proper app shell with:
- Skip link (already exists)
- Header with quick capture input (on every authenticated page)
- Nav with GTD list links + inbox count badge
- Main content area
- Logout

The base template should conditionally show the full nav only for authenticated pages. The welcome/login/register pages should keep their simpler layout.

### One Watch-Out

The current index route (`src/routes/index.rs`) has session-aware logic (redirect to /todos if logged in, show welcome page if not). This needs to evolve — authenticated users should see the dashboard instead of /todos. Make sure the existing e2e tests still pass after this change, or update them to reflect the new flow.

I'll be watching for test-first discipline. Write the failing test, then make it pass, then refactor. No production code without a failing test. Let me know if you have questions about the testing approach.
input.summary: TDD guidance for Nav + Dashboard: test sequence and strategy

## TDD Strategy for Nav + Dashboard

This is primarily a structural/UI task, not a domain logic task. That shifts where the tests live — more integration tests and e2e tests, fewer domain unit tests.

4. `dashboard_shows_summary_counts` — response contains inbox count, next actions count, etc. (all zero for a fresh user)

2. **Green**: Add a dashboard route that redirects unauthenticated users. Decide: does / become the dashboard, or does the welcome page remain for unauthenticated users and dashboard is at /dashboard?

6. **Green**: Add the nav links to the template

8. **Green**: Add the placeholder route and template
9. **Repeat** for each placeholder route
10. **Refactor**: Extract the nav into a shared template component (base.html block)

content.text:
TDD guidance sent to Luca. Key points:

- **Integration tests first**: dashboard auth redirect, 200 for authenticated user, nav links present, summary counts, nav on all authenticated pages
- **Placeholder routes**: GTD routes can start as stubs returning 200 with nav
- **Red-Green-Refactor sequence**: 10 small steps from "dashboard route doesn't exist" to "nav extracted into shared template"
- **Watch-out**: Current index route redirects to /todos — needs to evolve to dashboard
- **No domain unit tests** for this task (it's structural, not domain logic)

Standing by to review Luca's work as it progresses.
